title: Layout Mode Property
text: Layout Mode Property The Layout Mode property is a component-level setting that determines how a DSP’s layout content is processed during HTML generation. It controls the interaction between custom HTML and the layout template system. By selecting a Layout Mode, you control three key aspects: Whether the HTML structure is generated automatically How integration with layout templates occurs Where component widget binding takes place Layout Mode Property Details The Layout Mode property is defined by the following attributes. Name: Layout Mode Scope: Component level Data Type: String Default Value: Full Document ( D ) IDE Location: Property Inspector (Component Properties section) Layout Mode Values The Layout Mode property accepts three values, each designed for different development scenarios: Value Mode Name Description G Generated Creates initial HTML structure with component widget binding points for widget rendering F Fragment DSP Layout content embeds within layout template structure D Full Document DSP Layout content is treated as complete HTML document (default) Generated Mode (G) Generated mode creates the complete HTML structure automatically. Your DSP Layout content is not used, and the layout template provides all HTML elements including the document structure and component widget binding points. This is the default mode for UX component widget. This mode is optimized for component widget development where you want automatic HTML generation while your widgets focus on rendering their specific content. Note: Generated mode is useful for component widgets other than U_NONE. Fragment Mode (F) Fragment mode combines your custom DSP Layout HTML with the layout template system. Your DSP Layout is treated as an HTML fragment and embedded within the structure provided by the layout template. The layout template supplies the <head> section, scripts, and overall document structure, while your DSP Layout provides the custom body content and component-specific HTML elements. Full Document Mode (D) Full Document mode uses your DSP Layout content exactly as provided, giving you complete control over the HTML structure. This is the default mode for classic DSP and maintains backward compatibility with existing DSPs. For contained DSPs in this mode, the <head> section is automatically removed to prevent duplication when the DSP is embedded within another page. For component widgets to render in Fragment and Full Document modes, you must include component binding placeholders in your DSP layout content. For more detailed information on the differences between main and contained DSPs across all layout modes, see Processing Flow . Behavior Differences The table below summarizes the differences in behavior for each layout mode. Aspect Generated (G) Fragment (F) Full Document (D) DSP Layout Usage Ignored Used as fragment Used as complete document HTML Generation Template provides all Template + your fragment Your content only Component Binding Automatic Manual placement Manual placement Template Integration Full Partial None Related Links Customizing Layout Template The layout template system provides a centralized mechanism for controlling the HTML structure of DSP applications. When a DSP request is processed, USYSHTTP invokes DSP.HANDLER->genResponse(), which calls the USYSHTTP_LAYOUT::GETHTMLLAYOUT global ProcScript library to generate the final HTML output. Layout Template System The layout template system provides a centralized mechanism for generating consistent initial HTML content—including <head> sections, scripts, and document structure—for DSP applications. This allows common elements to be managed in one location while individual DSPs contribute their specific content. UX Widget Interface Web Widgets define the UX and UI of your web application. With UX Widget Interfaces you can implement your own UX Widgets, with layout constructs that match your preferred UI design. weblayout Copies the layout of the dynamic server page instance and prepares to send it to the browser.
